title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR TRAINING A GENERATIVE ADVERSE NETWORK USING IMAGES THAT INCLUDE REPRESENTATIONS OF AN ANOMALY; SISTEMA Y MÉTODO PARA ENTRENAR UNA RED GENERATIVA ANTAGÓNICA USANDO IMÁGENES QUE INCLUYEN REPRESENTACIONES DE UNA ANOMALÍA
abstract:
La presente invención se refiere a sistemas y métodos implementados por computadora para el entrenamiento y uso de redes generativas antagónicas. En una implementación, un sistema para entrenar una red generativa antagónica puede incluir al menos un procesador que puede proveer una pluralidad de imágenes que incluyen representaciones de una característica de interés e indicadores de ubicación de la característica de interés y el uso de la primera pluralidad de indicadores para entrenar una red de detección de objetos. Además, el o los procesadores pueden proveer una segunda pluralidad de imágenes que incluye representaciones de la característica de interés, y aplicar la red de detección de objetos entrenada a la segunda pluralidad para producir una pluralidad de detecciones de la característica de interés. Adicionalmente, el o los procesadores pueden proveer verificaciones establecidas manualmente de verdaderos positivos y falsos positivos con respecto a la pluralidad de detecciones, usar las verificaciones para entrenar una red generativa antagónica, y volver a entrenar la red generativa antagónica usando por lo menos un conjunto adicional de imágenes, detecciones adicionales y otras verificaciones establecidas manualmente.

The present invention relates to computer-implemented systems and methods for training and using generative adversarial networks. In one implementation, a system for training a generative adversarial network may include at least one processor that can provide a plurality of images that include representations of a feature of interest and location indicators of the feature of interest and using the first plurality of indicators to train an object detection network. In addition, the processor(s) may provide a second plurality of images that include representations of the feature of interest, and apply the trained object detection network to the second plurality to produce a plurality of detections of the feature of interest. Additionally, the processor(s) may provide manually established checks for true positives and false positives with respect to the plurality of detections, use the checks to train a generative adversarial network, and retrain the generative adversarial network using at least one additional set of images, additional detections, and other manually established checks.
claims:

title: AN INTELLIGENT SEGMENTATION METHOD OF ULTRASOUND IMAGE BASED ON AUTOMATIC CONTEXT AND DATA AUGMENTATION; 基于自动上下文和数据增强的超声图像智能分割方法
abstract:
本发明基于自动上下文和数据增强的超声图像智能分割方法。本发明首先对超声图像数据集进行一系列图像预处理，得到预处理后的数据集；接着，对预处理后的数据集进行数据增强，扩大数据集规模，得到扩增数据集；然后将扩增数据集输入到基于自动上下文的全卷积神经网络中，以端到端的方式训练模型，实现扩增数据集的初步分割；最后，对初步分割结果进行精细化后处理。本发明具有分割准确率高、鲁棒性和泛化性强、分割边缘平滑性好等优点，在有限训练数据集情况下也能获得理想的分割效果。

The present invention is an ultrasonic image intelligent segmentation method based on automatic context and data enhancement. The invention first performs a series of image preprocessing on the ultrasound image data set to obtain the preprocessed data set; then, the data set after the preprocessing is enhanced to expand the scale of the data set to obtain the expanded data set; and then the expanded data set is obtained. The augmented data set is input into a fully convolutional neural network based on automatic context, and the model is trained in an end-to-end manner to realize the preliminary segmentation of the augmented data set; finally, the preliminary segmentation results are refined and post-processed. The invention has the advantages of high segmentation accuracy, strong robustness and generalization, good segmentation edge smoothness and the like, and can also obtain an ideal segmentation effect in the case of limited training data sets.
claims:
1 .基于自动上下文和数据增强的超声图像智能分割方法，其特征在于，包括如下步骤： (1)首先对超声图像数据集进行预处理，得到预处理后的数据集； 分别对超声数据集进行间隔为m天的多次标注，取多次标注的平均值作为分割的标签; 针对超声图像的高噪声、低对比度、弱边界、不均匀特性，进行去噪处理，去噪处理包括:首 先截取超声图像感兴趣区域，使用双边滤波器进行滤波处理，在保留图像中边缘信息的同 时去除噪声，进行直方图均衡化增强图像的对比度，使用均值漂移滤波改善超声图像的均 匀性;具体包括如下步骤:步骤1：打标签，包括以下步骤： 1.1 技术人员标注；由多位技术人员分别对超声数据集进行间隔为m天，m取大于等于2 的整数，的多次标注，m为设定值； 1.2 确定标签;取多位技术人员多次标注的平均值作为分割的标签； 步骤2：图像预处理，包括以下步骤： 2.1 截取感兴趣区域;从原始超声图像中截取包含完整缺陷区域且缺陷区域居中的子 图像，即感兴趣区域； 2.2 双边滤波;对感兴趣区域使用双边滤波器完成去噪，得到去噪图像； 2.3 直方图均衡化;对去噪图像进行直方图均衡化来增强对比度得到直方图均衡化的 图像； 2.4 均值漂移;对直方图均衡化的图像使用均值漂移滤波器来改善均匀性； (2)接着，对预处理后的超声数据集进行数据增强，扩大数据集规模，得到扩增数据集； 具体包括:步骤21：缩放;按照设定的比例缩小或者放大原始图像，其中缩放比例的大小采 用随机或人为定义的方式给出，得到缩放图像； 步骤22：水平、垂直翻转;沿着水平或者垂直方向翻转缩放图像，得到翻转图像； 步骤23：平滑形变;使用随机位移矢量在nXn，0 2 .根据权利要求1所述的基于自动上下文和数据增强的超声图像智能分割方法，其特 征在于，步骤⑷包括:首先，去除分割结果中由于高噪声而误判的缺陷区域，提升缺陷区域 分割结果的准确率和可靠性;然后,对分割结果进行形态学闭运算和开运算处理，改善分割 结果的边缘平滑性，实现对缺陷分割结果的精细化处理。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的基于自动上下文和数据增强的超声图像智能分割方法，其特 征在于，步骤(4)具体包括如下步骤:步骤1：去除误判缺陷区域;使用OpenCV计算所有判别 为缺陷区域的面积大小，去除上述面积过小的误判缺陷区域，得到去除误判区域的分割结 果； 步骤2：形态学闭运算和开运算处理，包括以下步骤： 2.1 闭运算;对去除误判区域的分割结果使用闭运算填充缺陷区域的空洞部分，连接狭 窄的缺口，得到闭运算处理的分割结果； 2.2 开运算;对比运算处理的分割结果使用开运算断开狭窄的缺口，消除缺陷区域边缘 的突出部分，平滑缺陷区域边缘，得到精细化后处理的分割结果。

Carry out interval to ultrasonic data set respectively For the multiple annotations of m days, the average value of the multiple annotations is taken as the segmentation label; for the high noise, low contrast, weak boundary, and uneven characteristics of the ultrasound image, denoising processing is performed, and the denoising processing includes: first intercepting the ultrasound For the region of interest in the image, use bilateral filter for filtering, remove noise while preserving edge information in the image, perform histogram equalization to enhance the contrast of the image, and use mean shift filtering to improve the uniformity of the ultrasound image; the specific steps include the following: Step 1: Labeling, including the following steps: 1.1 Marking by technicians; multiple technicians mark the ultrasound data set at intervals of m days, where m is an integer greater than or equal to 2, and m is the set value; 1.2 Determine the label; take the average value of multiple annotations by multiple technicians as the label for segmentation; Step 2: Image preprocessing, including the following steps: 2.1 Intercept the region of interest; intercept the sub-image containing the complete defect area and the center of the defect area from the original ultrasound image, that is, the area of interest; 2.2 Bilateral filtering; use bilateral filtering to denoise the region of interest to obtain a denoised image; 2.3 Histogram equalization; perform histogram equalization on the denoised image to enhance the contrast to obtain a histogram equalized image; 2.4 Mean shift; use a mean shift filter to improve the uniformity of the histogram equalized image; (2) Next, perform data enhancement on the preprocessed ultrasound data set, expand the scale of the data set, and obtain an enlarged data set; Including: step 21: scaling; reducing or enlarging the original image according to a set ratio, wherein the size of the scaling ratio is given in a random or artificially defined manner, to obtain a scaled image; Step 22: flipping horizontally and vertically; horizontally or vertically Flip and zoom the image in the direction to obtain a flipped image; Step 23: Smooth deformation; use a random displacement vector at nXn, 0 2 . the ultrasonic image intelligent segmentation method based on automatic context and data enhancement according to claim 1, is characterized in that, step (4) comprises: at first, remove the defect area that is wrongly judged due to high noise in the segmentation result, improve the defect area segmentation result Then, perform morphological closing and opening operations on the segmentation results to improve the edge smoothness of the segmentation results and realize the refined processing of the defect segmentation results. 3 . the ultrasonic image intelligent segmentation method based on automatic context and data enhancement according to claim 1, is characterized in that, step (4) specifically comprises the steps: Step 1: remove misjudgment defect area; Use OpenCV to calculate all judgments as defects The area size of the area, remove the above-mentioned misjudged defect area that is too small, and obtain the segmentation result of removing the misjudged area; Step 2: Morphological closing operation and opening operation processing, including the following steps: 2.1 Closing operation; use closing operation to fill the hollow part of the defect area for the segmentation result of removing the misjudged area, connect the narrow gap, and obtain the segmentation result of closing operation processing; 2.2 Open operation; compare the segmentation results of the operation processing. Use the open operation to break the narrow gap, eliminate the protruding part of the edge of the defect area, smooth the edge of the defect area, and obtain the segmentation result of refined post-processing.

title: CARBON FIBER COMPOSITE CORE WIRE DAMAGE DETECTION METHOD BASED ON SEMI-SUPERVISED ABNORMAL DETECTION; 基于半监督异常检测的碳纤维复合芯导线损伤检测方法
abstract:
本发明公开了基于半监督异常检测的碳纤维复合芯导线损伤检测方法，包括以下步骤：S1：采用X射线成像的方式对碳纤维复合芯导线进行图像采集并制作数据集；S2：搭建卷积神经网络；S3：使用训练样本训练步骤S2中搭建好的卷积神经网络，每训练一轮，便用模型对检测样本进行检测，判断输入图像中是否有损伤部分；S4：选取步骤S4中效果最好的一轮作为最终结果。

The invention discloses a carbon fiber composite core wire damage detection method based on semi-supervised abnormal detection, including the following steps: S1: adopting X-ray imaging to image the carbon fiber composite core wire and making a data set; S2: building a convolutional neural network ; S3: Use the training samples to train the convolutional neural network built in step S2. After each training round, use the model to detect the detection samples to determine whether there is damage in the input image; S4: select the best effect in step S4 The round as the final result.
claims:
1.一种基于半监督异常检测的碳纤维复合芯导线损伤检测方法，其特征在于，该方法 包括如下步骤： S1：采用X射线成像的方式对碳纤维复合芯导线进行图像采集； S2 ：利用S1中采集到的图像制作数据集，并选择训练样本和测试样本； S3：根据生成器子网络G(x)与判别器子网络D(x)搭建卷积神经网络； S4：使用训练样本训练步骤S3中搭建好的卷积神经网络，每训练一轮，便用模型对检测 样本进行检测，判断输入图像中是否有损伤部分； S5：选取步骤S4中效果最好一轮对应的模型作为最终结果。 2.根据权利要求1所述的一种基于半监督异常检测的碳纤维复合芯导线损伤检测方 法，其特征在于，步骤S2的方法如下： S 2.1：对碳纤维复合芯导线X光图像进行裁剪，去除图像中导线以外的部分，并将导线 部分裁成预设的大小； S 2.2：将S2.1中裁好的碳纤维复合芯导线X光图像分为有损与无损两类； S 2.3：划分训练集与测试集，训练集中为无损的碳纤维复合芯导线X光图像，测试集中 为无损碳纤维复合芯导线X光图像和有损的碳纤维复合芯导线X光图像。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的一种基于半监督异常检测的碳纤维复合芯导线损伤检测方 法，其特征在于，步骤S3的生成器子网络G (x)与判别器子网络D(x)具体处理方法如下： 生成器子网络的具体工作流程为:将128*64*1的碳纤维复合芯导线X光图像作为输入， 经过第一层卷积层后得到32*32*64的特征图，之后再依次经过下采样块1、2、3、4分别得到 16*16*128、8*8*256、4*4*512、2*2*512的特征图，之后再经过Relu层激活，激活后经过一层 卷积层得到1*1*512的特征图，至此，下采样提取特征完成;将下采样得到的1*1*512的特征 图通过上采样块5得到2*2*512的特征图，将此特征图与之前经过下采样块4得到的2*2*512 的特征图在“通道”维度上连接得到2*2*1024的特征图；之后将此2*2*1024的特征图再经过 上采样块4得到4*4*512的特征图，将该特征图与之前经过下采样3得到的4*4*512的特征图 在“通道”维度上连接得到4*4*1024的特征图，之后将此4*4*1024的特征图再经过上采样块 3得到8*8*256的特征图，将该特征图与之前经过下采样2得到的8*8*256的特征图在“通道” 维度上连接得到8*8*512的特征图；之后将此8*8*512的特征图再经过上采样块2得到16* 16*128的特征图，将该特征图与之前经过下采样1得到的16*16*128的特征图在“通道”维度 上连接得到16*16*256的特征图；之后将此16*16*256的特征图再经过上采样块1得到32* 32*64的特征图，将该特征图与之前经过第一层卷积层得到的32*32*64的特征图在“通道” 维度上连接得到32*32*128的特征图，之后此32*32*128的特征图再经过一层转置卷积层能 得到128*64*1的特征图，最后经过一层Tanh层，至此图像重构完成； 判别器子网络的具体工作流程为： 将生成器子网络的生成图像和碳纤维复合芯导线X光图像原图作为输入到判别器子网 络中，输入图像大小为128*64*1，输入图像先经过一层卷积层得到32*32*64的特征图，之后 通过一层Relu激活，之后依次经过特征提取块1、2、3,分别得到16*16*128、8*8*256、4*4* 512的特征图，之后连续经过两层卷积层得到2*2*100，1*1*1的特征图，最后经过sigmoid层 得到最终的概率结果。 4 .根据权利要求1所述的一种基于半监督异常检测的碳纤维复合芯导线损伤检测方 X2、 法,其特征在于,步骤S4训练的具体方法如下： (1)从训练集中随机选取512张图像｛χι、Χ2、 (X)对这些图片重构得到｛yi、y2、……>y 5 i 2 ｝； (2)将｛χι、Χ2、……X512｝、｛yi、y2、……>y 512 ｝输入到判别器子网络D (x)中，D (x)给出这些 图是真图的概率切如、Px z 、......、Px slz )、｛pyi、Py z > ......、Py slz ；： (3)利用梯度下降算法减小损失函数函数值，优化生成器子网络，使用的优化器为亚当 优化器、设定学习率，使用的损失函数为LG = Ladv + Lcon,其中，= X512｝输入到生成器子网络中G(X)中，G £啬10g(l - 1. A carbon fiber composite core wire damage detection method based on semi-supervised abnormal detection, characterized in that the method includes the following steps: S1: Use X-ray imaging to image the carbon fiber composite core wire; S2: Use the images collected in S1 to make a data set, and select training samples and test samples; S3: Build a convolutional neural network based on the generator sub-network G(x) and the discriminator sub-network D(x); S4: Use the training samples to train the convolutional neural network built in step S3. title: AN ABNORMAL DETECTION METHOD AND SYSTEM OF CHEST RADIOGRAPH IMAGE BASED ON DEEP LEARNING; 一种基于深度学习的胸片图像异常检测方法及系统
abstract:
本发明公开了一种基于深度学习的胸片图像异常检测方法及系统，所述方法包括以下步骤：构建基础辨识算法；获取胸片图像，并依据基础辨识算法对胸片图像进行学习，生成分类模型；依据分类模型对待检测的胸片图像进行分类，得到异常胸片图像和正常胸片图像；对异常胸片图像进行标注，完成检测。通过构建基础辨识算法对胸片图像进行深度学习，从而生成分类模型，能够对待检测的胸片图像直接进行分类，得到正常胸片图像和异常胸片图像，然后将异常胸片图像发送给医生，正常胸片图像不发送给医生，从而减少了医生的工作内容，提高医生的工作效率；此外，通过对异常胸片图像进行标注，能够让医生直观地观察到胸片图像异常的位置和原因。

The present invention discloses a method and system for detecting abnormalities of chest radiograph images based on deep learning. The method includes the following steps: constructing a basic identification algorithm; acquiring a chest radiograph image, and learning the chest radiograph image according to the basic identification algorithm to generate a classification Model; classify the chest radiograph images to be detected according to the classification model to obtain abnormal chest radiograph images and normal chest radiograph images; mark the abnormal chest radiograph images to complete the detection. By constructing a basic recognition algorithm to perform in-depth learning on chest radiograph images, a classification model can be generated. The chest radiograph images to be detected can be directly classified to obtain normal chest radiograph images and abnormal chest radiograph images, and then the abnormal chest radiograph images are sent to the doctor. Normal chest X-ray images are not sent to the doctor, thereby reducing the work content of the doctor and improving the doctor's work efficiency; in addition, by marking the abnormal chest X-ray image, the doctor can intuitively observe the location and cause of the abnormal chest X-ray image.

title: A METHOD FOR TERAHERTZ CONTINUOUS WAVE EXTENDED PHASE TOMOGRAPHY IN ELECTRONICS; 一种电子学太赫兹连续波扩展相位层析成像方法
abstract:
本发明涉及一种电子学太赫兹连续波扩展相位层析成像方法。该方法包括太赫兹扩展相位层析成像数据采集1和太赫兹扩展相位层析成像图像重建2，由太赫兹扩展相位数据采集装置3采集未扩展相位，并经太赫兹扩展相位提取方法4提取扩展相位，再由扩展相位数据预处理5实现投影偏移修正和高斯平滑降噪，形成基于高斯模型修正的扩展相位数据，基于该数据实现扩展相位图像重建6。该方法对太赫兹波束与样品互作用的相位变化具有高灵敏度，且对投影数据作了去噪和修正处理，可实现高清晰度成像，成像性能优于幅度成像。

The present invention relates to an electronic terahertz continuous-wave extended phase tomography method. The method includes terahertz extended phase tomography data acquisition 1 and terahertz extended phase tomography image reconstruction 2. A terahertz extended phase data acquisition device 3 acquires the unextended phase, and a terahertz extended phase extraction method 4 extracts the extended phase. Extended phase data preprocessing 5 then implements projection offset correction and Gaussian smoothing noise reduction to generate Gaussian model-corrected extended phase data. Extended phase image reconstruction 6 is then performed based on this data. This method is highly sensitive to phase changes caused by the interaction between the terahertz beam and the sample, and denoises and corrects the projection data, enabling high-definition imaging with superior imaging performance compared to amplitude imaging.

title: METHOD FOR DETECTING AN ANOMALOUS IMAGE AMONG A FIRST DATASET OF IMAGES USING AN ADVERSARIAL AUTOENCODER; PROCÉDÉ PERMETTANT DE DÉTECTER UNE IMAGE ANORMALE PARMI UN PREMIER ENSEMBLE DE DONNÉES D'IMAGES À L'AIDE D'UN AUTO-ENCODEUR CONTRADICTOIRE; VERFAHREN ZUM ERFASSEN EINES ANOMALEN BILDES UNTER EINEM ERSTEN DATENSATZ VON BILDERN MIT EINEM KONTRADIKTORISCHEN AUTOENCODER
claims:
Computer-implemented method for detecting an anomalous image among a first dataset of images using an Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) wherein the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is trained in a first training with a training dataset of images (11), whereas the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is optimized such that a distribution of latent representations (13) of images of the training dataset of images (11) approaches a predetermined prior distribution (17) and that a reconstruction error of reconstructed images (15) of the training dataset of images is minimized, after the first training of the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16), anomalies are detected in the latent representations (13) and the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is trained in a second training with the training dataset of images (11), but taking into account the detected anomalies, wherein anomalies detected in the latent representation (13) are taken into account either by excluding detected anomalies from the training set (11), or by using a weighted loss function for tuning the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16), whereas anomalies detected in the latent representation (13) receive a reduced weight, or by modifying a reconstruction target for each of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation (13) to a noise-corrupted version of itself, or by modifying a reconstruction target for each of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation (13) to an image close or closest in the latent space that is identified as no anomaly, the anomalous image among the first dataset of images is detected by the trained Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) dependent on at least one of the reconstruction error of the image and the probability density under the predetermined prior distribution (17). Method according to claim 1, characterized in that the detection of the anomalies in the latent representation (13) is done using a one-class support vector machine and the detection of the anomalies in the latent representation (13) is done dependent on an user-defined anomaly rate, wherein a decision boundary of the one-class support vector machine depends on the user-defined anomaly rate. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the detection of the anomalies in the latent representation (13) is done dependent on at least one of their reconstruction error of their images and dependent on their probability density under the predetermined prior distribution (17). Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the first training and the second training are repeatedly conducted consecutively for a certain number of repetitions or until a training target is reached. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the first training comprises a first predetermined number of iterations. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the second training comprises a second predetermined number of iterations. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the predetermined prior distribution includes at least one dedicated rejection class. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that images of the training dataset of images (11) are initially unlabeled. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the method includes a query to a user to identify a presented image as normal or anomaly. Method according to claim 9, characterized in that the detected anomalies in the latent representation (13) taken into account in the second training include the user-identified anomalies. Method according to claim 9 or claim 10, characterized in that the detection of the anomalies in the latent representation (13) is done using a one-class support vector machine or a local outlier factor algorithm and whereas for a training of the one-class Support vector machine or the local outlier factor algorithm images of the training dataset of images (11), which are identified by the user as normal or as anomalies, are excluded. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that an anomaly score for images in the first dataset of images is output. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that an anomaly score for images in the training dataset (11) of images is output. Method according to claim 9, characterized in that images presented to the user are selected dependent on their anomaly score. Method according to one of claims 12 to 14, characterized in that the anomaly score is calculated based on at least one of a reconstruction error and a probability density under the predetermined prior distribution (17). Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that it is used in visual quality inspection, medical image analysis, visual surveillance, or automated driving. Computer program, adapted to carry out a method according to one of claims 1 to 15. Computing device, adapted to carry out a method according to one of claims 1 to 15.

Computerimplementiertes Verfahren zum Detektieren eines anomalen Bildes unter einem ersten Datensatz von Bildern unter Verwendung eines Adversarial-Autoencoders (12, 14, 16), wobei der Adversarial-Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) in einem ersten Training mit einem Trainingsdatensatz von Bildern (11) trainiert wird, wobei der Adversarial-Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) derart optimiert wird, dass sich eine Verteilung latenter Repräsentationen (13) von Bildern des Trainingsdatensatzes von Bildern (11) einer vorbestimmten A-priori-Verteilung (17) annähert und dass ein Rekonstruktionsfehler rekonstruierter Bilder (15) des Trainingsdatensatzes von Bildern minimiert wird, nach dem ersten Training des Adversarial-Autoencoders (12, 14, 16) Anomalien in den latenten Repräsentationen (13) detektiert werden und der Adversarial-Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) in einem zweiten Training mit dem Trainingsdatensatz von Bildern (11) trainiert wird, aber unter Berücksichtigung der detektierten Anomalien, wobei Anomalien, die in der latenten Repräsentation (13) detektiert werden, berücksichtigt werden entweder durch Ausschließen detektierter Anomalien aus dem Trainingssatz (11), oder durch Verwenden einer gewichteten Verlustfunktion zum Abstimmen des Adversarial-Autoencoders (12, 14, 16), wobei in der latenten Repräsentation (13) detektierte Anomalien ein reduziertes Gewicht erhalten, oder durch Modifizieren eines Rekonstruktionsziels für jede eines Satzes der Anomalien, die in der latenten Repräsentation (13) detektiert werden, zu einer rauschbehafteten Version von sich selbst, oder durch Modifizieren eines Rekonstruktionsziels für jede eines Satzes von Anomalien, die in der latenten Repräsentation (13) detektiert werden, zu einem Bild nahe oder am nächsten in dem latenten Raum, das als ohne Anomalie identifiziert wird, wobei das anomale Bild unter dem ersten Datensatz von Bildern durch den trainierten Adversarial-Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) in Abhängigkeit von wenigstens einem des Rekonstruktionsfehlers des Bildes und der Wahrscheinlichkeitsdichte unter der vorbestimmten A-priori-Verteilung (17) detektiert wird. Verfahren nach Anspruch 1, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass die Detektion der Anomalien in der latenten Repräsentation (13) unter Verwendung einer Einzelklasse-Support-Vector-Machine erfolgt und die Detektion der Anomalien in der latenten Repräsentation (13) in Abhängigkeit von einer benutzerdefinierten Anomalierate erfolgt, wobei eine Entscheidungsgrenze der Einzelklasse-Support-Vector-Machine von der benutzerdefinierten Anomalierate abhängt. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass die Detektion der Anomalien in der latenten Repräsentation (13) in Abhängigkeit von wenigstens einem ihres Rekonstruktionsfehlers ihrer Bilder und in Abhängigkeit von ihrer Wahrscheinlichkeitsdichte unter der vorbestimmten A-priori-Verteilung (17) erfolgt. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass das erste Training und das zweite Training konsekutiv für eine gewisse Anzahl an Wiederholungen oder bis zum Erreichen eines Trainingsziels wiederholt durchgeführt werden. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass das erste Training eine erste vorbestimmte Anzahl an Iterationen umfasst. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass das zweite Training eine zweite vorbestimmte Anzahl an Iterationen umfasst. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass die vorbestimmte A-priori-Verteilung wenigstens eine dedizierte Abweisungsklasse beinhaltet. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass Bilder des Trainingsdatensatzes von Bildern (11) anfangs unmarkiert sind. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass das Verfahren eine Anfrage an einen Benutzer zum Identifizieren eines präsentierten Bildes als normal oder anormal beinhaltet. Verfahren nach Anspruch 9, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass die detektierten Anomalien in der latenten Repräsentation (13), die in dem zweiten Training berücksichtigt werden, die benutzeridentifizierten Anomalien beinhalten. Verfahren nach Anspruch 9 oder 10, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass die Detektion der Anomalien in der latenten Repräsentation (13) unter Verwendung einer Einzelklasse-Support-Vector-Machine oder eines Lokaler-Ausreißerfaktor-Algorithmus erfolgt, und wobei für ein Training der Einzelklasse-Support-Vector-Machine oder des Lokaler-Ausreißerfaktor-Algorithmus Bilder des Trainingsdatensatzes von Bildern (11), die durch den Benutzer als normal oder als Anomalien identifiziert werden, ausgeschlossen werden. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass eine Anomaliebewertung für Bilder in dem ersten Datensatz von Bildern ausgegeben wird. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass eine Anomaliebewertung für Bilder in dem Trainingsdatensatz (11) von Bildern ausgegeben wird. Verfahren nach Anspruch 9, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass Bilder, die dem Benutzer präsentiert werden, in Abhängigkeit von ihrer Anomaliebewertung ausgewählt werden. Verfahren nach einem der Ansprüche 12 bis 14, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass die Anomaliebewertung basierend auf wenigstens einem eines Rekonstruktionsfehlers und einer Wahrscheinlichkeitsdichte unter der vorbestimmten A-priori-Verteilung (17) berechnet wird. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass es bei einer visuellen Qualitätsinspektion, medizinischen Bildanalyse, visuellen Überwachung oder bei automatisiertem Fahren verwendet wird. Computerprogramm, das zum Ausführen eines Verfahrens nach einem der Ansprüche 1 bis 15 eingerichtet ist. Rechenvorrichtung, die zum Ausführen eines Verfahrens nach einem der Ansprüche 1 bis 15 eingerichtet ist.

Procédé mis en œuvre par ordinateur pour détecter une image anormale parmi un premier ensemble de données d'images à l'aide d'un auto-encodeur contradictoire (12, 14, 16) dans lequel l'auto-encodeur contradictoire (12, 14, 16) est entraîné lors d'un premier apprentissage avec un ensemble de données d'apprentissage d'images (11), l'auto-encodeur contradictoire (12, 14, 16) étant optimisé de telle sorte qu'une distribution de représentations latentes (13) d'images de l'ensemble de données d'apprentissage d'images (11) se rapproche d'une distribution préalable prédéterminée (17) et qu'une erreur de reconstruction d'images reconstruites (15) de l'ensemble de données d'apprentissage d'images soit minimisée, après le premier apprentissage de l'auto-encodeur contradictoire (12, 14, 16), des anomalies sont détectées dans les représentations latentes (13) et l'auto-encodeur contradictoire (12, 14, 16) est entraîné lors d'un second apprentissage avec l'ensemble de données d'apprentissage d'images (11), mais en tenant compte des anomalies détectées, dans lequel des anomalies détectées dans la représentation latente (13) sont prises en compte en excluant des anomalies détectées de l'ensemble d'apprentissage (11), ou utilisant une fonction de perte pondérée pour régler l'auto-encodeur contradictoire (12, 14, 16), les anomalies détectées dans la représentation latente (13) recevant un poids réduit, ou modifiant une cible de reconstruction pour chacune d'un ensemble des anomalies détectées dans la représentation latente (13) en une version corrompue par le bruit d'elle-même, ou modifiant une cible de reconstruction pour chacune d'un ensemble des anomalies détectées dans la représentation latente (13) en une image proche ou la plus proche dans l'espace latent qui est identifiée comme n'étant pas une anomalie, l'image anormale parmi le premier ensemble de données d'images est détectée par l'auto-encodeur contradictoire entraîné (12, 14, 16) en fonction au moins de l'erreur de reconstruction de l'image et de la densité de probabilité en vertu de la distribution préalable prédéterminée (17). Procédé selon la revendication 1, caractérisé en ce que la détection des anomalies dans la représentation latente (13) est réalisée à l'aide d'une machine à vecteur de support de classe unique et la détection des anomalies dans la représentation latente (13) est réalisée en fonction d'un taux d'anomalies défini par l'utilisateur, dans lequel une limite de décision de la machine à vecteur de support de classe unique dépend du taux d'anomalies défini par l'utilisateur. Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce que la détection des anomalies dans la représentation latente (13) est réalisée en fonction au moins d'une erreur de reconstruction de leurs images et en fonction de leur densité de probabilité en vertu de la distribution préalable prédéterminée (17). Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce que le premier apprentissage et le second apprentissage sont réalisés de manière répétée consécutivement pendant un certain nombre de répétitions ou jusqu'à ce qu'une cible d'apprentissage soit atteinte. Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce que le premier apprentissage comprend un premier nombre prédéterminé d'itérations. Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce que le second apprentissage comprend un second nombre prédéterminé d'itérations. Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce que la distribution préalable prédéterminée comporte au moins une classe de rejet dédiée. Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce que des images de l'ensemble de données d'apprentissage d'images (11) sont initialement non étiquetées. Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce qu' il comporte une requête adressée à un utilisateur pour identifier une image présentée comme normale ou anormale. Procédé selon la revendication 9, caractérisé en ce que les anomalies détectées dans la représentation latente (13) prises en compte dans le second apprentissage comporte les anomalies identifiées par l'utilisateur. Procédé selon la revendication 9 ou la revendication 10, caractérisé en ce que la détection des anomalies dans la représentation latente (13) est réalisée à l'aide d'une machine à vecteur de support de classe unique ou d'un algorithme de facteur d'aberration local et dans lequel pour un apprentissage de la machine à vecteur de support de classe unique ou de l'algorithme de facteur d'aberration local des images de l'ensemble de données d'apprentissage d'images (11), qui sont identifiées par l'utilisateur comme normales ou anormales, sont exclues. Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce qu' un score d'anomalies d'images du premier ensemble de données d'images est généré. Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce qu' un score d'anomalies d'images dans l'ensemble de données d'apprentissage (11) d'images est délivré en sortie. Procédé selon la revendication 9, caractérisé en ce que des images présentées à l'utilisateur sont sélectionnées en fonction de leur score d'anomalies. Procédé selon l'une des revendications 12 à 14, caractérisé en ce que le score d'anomalies est calculé en fonction au moins d'une erreur de reconstruction et d'une densité de probabilité en vertu de la distribution préalable prédéterminée (17). Procédé selon l'une des revendications précédentes, caractérisé en ce qu' il est utilisé dans l'inspection visuelle de la qualité, l'analyse d'images médicales, la surveillance visuelle ou la conduite automatisée. Programme informatique, conçu pour réaliser un procédé selon l'une des revendications 1 à 15. Dispositif informatique, conçu pour réaliser un procédé selon l'une des revendications 1 à 15.
solution_context: Computer-implemented method for detecting an anomalous image among a first dataset of images using an Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) wherein the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is trained in a first training with a training dataset of images (11), whereas the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is optimized such that a distribution of latent representations (13) of images of the training dataset of images (11) approaches a predetermined prior distribution (17) and that a reconstruction error of reconstructed images (15) of the training dataset of images is minimized, after the first training of the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16), anomalies are detected in the latent representations (13) and the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is trained in a second training with the training dataset of images (11), but taking into account the detected anomalies, wherein anomalies detected in the latent representation (13) are taken into account either by excluding detected anomalies from the training set (11), or by using a weighted loss function for tuning the Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16), whereas anomalies detected in the latent representation (13) receive a reduced weight, or by modifying a reconstruction target for each of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation (13) to a noise-corrupted version of itself, or by modifying a reconstruction target for each of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation (13) to an image close or closest in the latent space that is identified as no anomaly, the anomalous image among the first dataset of images is detected by the trained Adversarial Autoencoder (12, 14, 16) dependent on at least one of the reconstruction error of the image and the probability density under the predetermined prior distribution (17). Method according to claim 1, characterized in that the detection of the anomalies in the latent representation (13) is done using a one-class support vector machine and the detection of the anomalies in the latent representation (13) is done dependent on an user-defined anomaly rate, wherein a decision boundary of the one-class support vector machine depends on the user-defined anomaly rate.

title: EXPLAINABILITY IN MACHINE-LEARNING-BASED ANATOMICAL ABNORMALITY DETECTION ALGORITHM; EXPLICABILITÉ DANS UN ALGORITHME DE DÉTECTION D'ANOMALIE ANATOMIQUE BASÉ SUR L'APPRENTISSAGE AUTOMATIQUE; ERKLÄRBARKEIT IN EINEM ALGORITHMUS ZUR ERKENNUNG VON ANATOMISCHEN ANOMALIEN AUF DER BASIS VON MASCHINELLEM LERNEN
abstract: Various examples of the disclosure relate to explain-ability of a machine learning algorithm for detecting at least one abnormality within an anatomical region of interest. A trained machine learning algorithm is used to process one or more images associated with at least one abnormality within an anatomical region of interest to determine one or more reasons for a further trained machine learning algorithm detecting the at least one abnormality in the one or more images.
claims: A computer-implemented method (3000), comprising: - obtaining (3100) one or more images associated with at least one abnormality (2310) within an anatomical region of interest; and, - determining (3200), using a first trained machine learning algorithm (2100) and based on the one or more images (2300), one or more reasons for a second trained machine learning algorithm (2200) detecting the at least one abnormality (2310) in the one or more images (2300). The computer-implemented method (3000) of claim 1, wherein the at least one abnormality (2310) comprises one or more lung nodules and the one or more reasons comprise at least one imaging characteristic associated with the one or more lung nodules. The computer-implemented method (3000) of claim 1 or 2, wherein the one or more images comprise one or more medical images depicting the anatomical region of interest. The computer-implemented method (3000) of claim 3, wherein the one or more images further comprise one or more feature maps (2230) representing the at least one abnormality (2310), and the second trained machine learning algorithm (2200) comprises a first module (2210), the method further comprising: - generating, using the first module (2210) of the second trained machine learning algorithm (2200), the one or more feature maps (2230) based on the one or more medical images. The computer-implemented method (3000) of claim 4, wherein the one or more images (2300) further comprise a segmented image (2400) depicting the at least one abnormality (2310), and the second trained machine learning algorithm (2200) further comprises a second module (2220) connectable to the first module (2210), the method further comprising: - determining, using the second module (2220) of the second trained machine learning algorithm (2200), the segmented image (2400) based on the one or more feature maps (2230). The computer-implemented method (3000) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more reasons comprises at least one of the following: - a respective size of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective location of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective margin morphology of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a count of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective growth rate of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective type of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective shape of each of the at least one abnormality (2310). A computer-implemented method (4000), comprising: - obtaining (4100) one or more training images associated with at least one abnormality (2310) within an anatomical region of interest; - determining (4200), using a first machine learning algorithm and based on the one or more training images, one or more predicted reasons for a second trained machine learning algorithm detecting the at least one abnormality (2310); - updating (4300) parameter values of the first machine learning algorithm based on a comparison between each of the one or more predicted reasons and a corresponding reference reason for the second trained machine learning algorithm detecting the at least one abnormality (2310). The computer-implemented method (4000) of claim 7, wherein the at least one abnormality (2310) comprises one or more lung nodules and the one or more predicted reasons and the corresponding reference reason respectively comprise at least one imaging characteristic associated with the one or more lung nodules. The computer-implemented method (4000) of claim 7 or 8, wherein the one or more training images comprise one or more medical images depicting the anatomical region of interest. The computer-implemented method (4000) of claim 9, wherein the one or more training images further comprise one or more feature maps representing the at least one abnormality (2310), and the second trained machine learning algorithm comprises a first module, the method further comprising: - generating, using the first module of the second trained machine learning algorithm, the one or more feature maps based on the one or more medical images. The computer-implemented method (4000) of claim 10, wherein the one or more training images further comprise a segmented image depicting the at least one abnormality (2310), and the second trained machine learning algorithm further comprises a second module connectable to the first module, the method further comprising: - determining, using the second module of the second trained machine learning algorithm, the segmented image based on the one or more feature maps. The computer-implemented method (4000) of any one of claims 7 to 11, further comprising: - obtaining a set of training images associated with the at least one abnormality (2310), wherein the set of training images comprises the one or more training images; - clustering the set of training images into at least one group of training images based on one or more characteristics associated with the at least one abnormality (2310); and, - determining, for each of the at least one group, at least one common reference reason for the second trained machine learning algorithm detecting the at least one abnormality (2310), based on the respective group of training images, wherein the corresponding reference reason comprises the at least one common reference reason. The computer-implemented method (4000) of any one of claims 7 to 12, wherein the one or more predicted reasons and the corresponding reference reason respectively comprise at least one of the following: - a respective size of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective location of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective margin morphology of each of the at least one abnormality, - a count of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective growth rate of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective type of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective shape of each of the at least one abnormality (2310). A computing device (9000) comprising a processor (9020) and a memory (9030), wherein upon loading and executing program code from the memory (9030), the processor (9020) is configured to perform the method (3000, 4000) according to any one of the preceding claims. A medical imaging device comprising the computing device (9000) of claim 14.

title: ABNORMALITY DETECTION METHOD, ABNORMALITY DETECTION PROGRAM, ABNORMALITY DETECTION DEVICE, SERVER DEVICE, AND INFORMATION PROCESSING METHOD; PROCÉDÉ DE DÉTECTION D'ANOMALIE, PROGRAMME DE DÉTECTION D'ANOMALIE, DISPOSITIF DE DÉTECTION D'ANOMALIE, DISPOSITIF SERVEUR ET PROCÉDÉ DE TRAITEMENT D'INFORMATIONS; 異常検出方法、異常検出プログラム、異常検出装置、サーバ装置及び情報処理方法
abstract:
Provided is a computer for acquiring a chest X-ray image (S50) and detecting a borderline in the acquired chest X-ray image using a model (S100). The model is constructed using machine learning before detection, sets, on the basis of the borderline, a third lung region that includes a first lung region overlapping a heart and/or a second lung region overlapping a liver in the acquired chest X-ray image (S200), extracts a blood vessel index that represents the size and/or the density of one lung blood vessel present in a region that includes the third lung region (S500), determines, on the basis of the blood vessel index and a reference index that uses an index previously extracted from a region equivalent to the region included in the third lung region of the chest X-ray image in a normal state using a method by which the blood vessel index is extracted, whether or not the region included in the third lung region is in an abnormal state (S700), and outputs information that represents determination when it is determined that the region included in the third lung region is in an abnormal state.

L'invention concerne un ordinateur servant à acquérir une image radiographique thoracique (S50) et détecter une limite dans l'image radiographique thoracique acquise à l'aide d'un modèle (S100). Le modèle est construit à l'aide d'un apprentissage automatique avant la détection, définit, sur la base de la limite, une troisième région pulmonaire qui comprend une première région pulmonaire chevauchant un cœur et/ou une seconde région pulmonaire chevauchant un foie dans l'image radiographique thoracique acquise (S200), extrait un indice de vaisseau sanguin qui représente la taille et/ou la densité d'un vaisseau sanguin pulmonaire présent dans une région qui comprend la troisième région pulmonaire (S500), détermine, sur la base de l'indice de vaisseau sanguin et d'un indice de référence qui utilise un indice précédemment extrait d'une région équivalente à la région comprise dans la troisième région pulmonaire de l'image radiographique thoracique dans un état normal à l'aide d'un procédé par lequel l'indice de vaisseau sanguin est extrait, si la région comprise dans la troisième région pulmonaire est dans un état anormal (S700), et fournit en sortie des informations qui représentent une détermination lorsqu'il est déterminé que la région comprise dans la troisième région pulmonaire est dans un état anormal.

コンピュータが、胸部X線画像を取得し(S50)、取得した胸部X線画像において、境界線を、モデルを用いて検出し(S100)、モデルは検出前に機械学習を用いて構築され、境界線を基に、取得した胸部X線画像において、心臓と重なる第1肺領域および肝臓と重なる第2肺領域の少なくとも一方を含む第3肺領域を設定し(S200)、第3肺領域に含まれる領域に存在する少なくとも1つの肺血管の太さおよび密度の少なくとも一方を表す血管指標を抽出し(S500)、血管指標と、血管指標を抽出した方法を用いて正常状態の胸部X線画像の第3肺領域に含まれる領域に相当する領域から予め抽出された指標を用いた基準指標と、に基づいて第3肺領域に含まれる領域が異常状態であるか否かを判定し(S700)、第3肺領域に含まれる領域が異常状態であると判定されると、判定を表す情報を出力する。
claims:
コンピュータが、胸部X線画像を取得し、 前記コンピュータが、前記取得した胸部X線画像において、X線透過率の範囲が異なる複数の解剖学的構造の画像の境界線を、モデルを用いて検出し、前記モデルは前記検出前に機械学習を用いて構築され、 前記コンピュータが、検出された前記境界線を基に、前記取得した胸部X線画像において、肺と心臓とが重なる第1肺領域および肺と肝臓とが重なる第2肺領域のうち少なくとも一方を含む第3肺領域を設定し、 前記コンピュータが、前記第3肺領域に含まれる領域に存在する少なくとも1つの肺血管の太さおよび密度の少なくとも一方を表す血管指標を抽出し、 前記コンピュータが、前記血管指標と、前記血管指標を抽出した方法を用いて正常状態の胸部X線画像の前記第3肺領域に含まれる領域に相当する領域から予め抽出された指標を用いた基準指標と、に基づいて前記第3肺領域に含まれる領域が異常状態であるか否かを判定し、 前記第3肺領域に含まれる領域が異常状態であると判定されると、前記コンピュータが、前記判定を表す情報を出力する、 異常検出方法。 前記検出では、左室陰影、下行大動脈陰影、左横隔膜ドーム陰影、椎体の左境界線、椎体の右境界線、右房陰影、右横隔膜ドーム陰影のうち少なくとも2つの境界線が検出され、 前記設定では、前記第1肺領域を含む前記第3肺領域が設定され、前記第1肺領域は、前記検出で検出された境界線のうち少なくとも2つで挟まれる領域により設定される、 請求項1に記載の異常検出方法。 前記検出では、前記左室陰影、前記下行大動脈陰影、前記左横隔膜ドーム陰影、前記椎体の左境界線、前記椎体の右境界線、前記右房陰影、前記右横隔膜ドーム陰影のうち少なくとも前記椎体の左境界線、前記左室陰影、前記左横隔膜ドーム陰影、前記椎体の右境界線、前記右房陰影の境界線が検出され、 前記検出では、前記右横隔膜ドーム陰影、前記右背側肺底陰影、前記椎体の右境界線のうち少なくとも前記右横隔膜ドーム陰影、前記右背側肺底陰影の境界線が検出され、 前記設定では、前記第2肺領域は、前記検出で検出された境界線のうち前記右横隔膜ドーム陰影、前記右背側肺底陰影で挟まれる領域により設定される、 請求項6に記載の異常検出方法。 前記検出では、前記右横隔膜ドーム陰影、前記右背側肺底陰影、前記椎体の右境界線の境界線が検出され、 前記設定では、前記第2肺領域は、前記検出で検出された前記右横隔膜ドーム陰影、前記右背側肺底陰影、前記椎体の右境界線で挟まれる領域により設定される、 請求項6または7に記載の異常検出方法。 title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR QUANTIFYING UNCERTAINTY IN REASONING ABOUT 2D AND 3D SPATIAL FEATURES WITH A COMPUTER MACHINE LEARNING ARCHITECTURE; SYSTÈME ET PROCÉDÉ PERMETTANT DE QUANTIFIER UNE INCERTITUDE DANS UN RAISONNEMENT CONCERNANT DES CARACTÉRISTIQUES SPATIALES 2D ET 3D AVEC UNE ARCHITECTURE D'APPRENTISSAGE MACHINE INFORMATIQUE
abstract:
This invention provides a system and method to propagate uncertainty information in a deep learning pipeline. It allows for the propagation of uncertainty information from one deep learning model to the next by fusing model uncertainty with the original imagery dataset. This approach results in a deep learning architecture where the output of the system contains not only the prediction, but also the model uncertainty information associated with that prediction. The embodiments herein improve upon existing deep learning-based models (CADe models) by providing the model with uncertainty/confidence information associated with (e.g. CADe) decisions. This uncertainty information can be employed in various ways, including (a) transmitting uncertainty from a first stage (or subsystem) of the machine learning system into a next (second) stage (or the next subsystem), and (b) providing uncertainty information to the end user in a manner that characterizes the uncertainty of the overall machine learning model.

La présente invention concerne un système et un procédé permettant de propager des informations d'incertitude dans un pipeline d'apprentissage profond. Elle permet la propagation d'informations d'incertitude d'un modèle d'apprentissage profond au suivant par fusion d'une incertitude de modèle avec l'ensemble de données d'imagerie d'origine. Cette approche conduit à une architecture d'apprentissage profond où la sortie du système contient non seulement la prédiction, mais également les informations d'incertitude de modèle associées à cette prédiction. Les modes de réalisation de la présente invention améliorent les modèles basés sur l'apprentissage profond (modèles CADe) existants en fournissant au modèle des informations d'incertitude/de confiance associées à des décisions (par exemple CADe). Ces informations d'incertitude peuvent être utilisées de diverses manières, comprenant (a) la transmission d'une incertitude d'un premier étage (ou sous-système) du système d'apprentissage machine dans un prochain (second) étage (ou le prochain sous-système), et (b) la fourniture d'informations d'incertitude à l'utilisateur final d'une manière qui caractérise l'incertitude du modèle global d'apprentissage machine.

title: METHOD FOR DETECTING AN ANOMALOUS IMAGE AMONG A FIRST DATASET OF IMAGES USING AN ADVERSARIAL AUTOENCODER
abstract: A method for detecting an anomalous image among a dataset of images using an Adversarial Autoencoder includes training an Adversarial Autoencoder in a first training with a training dataset of images, with the Adversarial Autoencoder being optimized such that a distribution of latent representations of images of the training dataset of images approaches a predetermined prior distribution and that a reconstruction error of reconstructed images of the training dataset of images is minimized. Subsequently, anomalies are detected in the latent representation and the Adversarial Autoencoder is trained in a second training with the training dataset of images, but taking into account the detected anomalies. The anomalous image among the first dataset of images is detected by the trained Adversarial Autoencoder dependent on at least one of the reconstruction error of the image and a probability density under the predetermined prior distribution.

and a reconstruction error of reconstructed images of the training dataset of images is minimized; after the first training, detecting anomalies in the latent representations of images of the training dataset of images; in a second training, training the Adversarial Autoencoder using the training dataset of images and taking into account the detected anomalies; and detecting an anomalous image among a first dataset of images by the trained Adversarial Autoencoder dependent on one or both of the following: a reconstruction error of the anomalous image and a probability density under the predetermined prior distribution; wherein: the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by excluding detected anomalies from the training set for the second training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by using a weighted loss function for tuning the Adversarial Autoencoder in at least one subsequent training, by which weighted loss function anomalies detected in the latent representation receive a reduced weight in the at least one subsequent training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by modifying a reconstruction target for each of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation to a noise-corrupted version of itself for at least one subsequent training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by modifying a reconstruction target for each anomaly of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation to an image, closest in terms of distance to the anomaly in the latent space, that is identified as not being anomalous in at least one subsequent training. 2. wherein: the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by excluding detected anomalies from the training set for the second training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by using a weighted loss function for tuning the Adversarial Autoencoder in at least one subsequent training, by which weighted loss function anomalies detected in the latent representation receive a reduced weight in the at least one subsequent training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by modifying a reconstruction target for each of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation to a noise-corrupted version of itself for at least one subsequent training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by modifying a reconstruction target for each anomaly of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation to an image, closest in terms of distance to the anomaly in the latent space, that is identified as not being anomalous in at least one subsequent training.

title: FAST AND LOW MEMORY USAGE CONVOLUTIONAL NEURAL NETWORK FOR TOMOSYNTHESIS DATA PROCESSING AND FEATURE DETECTION
abstract: A convolutional neural network (CNN) is employed in an automated feature and/or anomaly detection system for analyzing images provided by X-ray imaging system. The automated detection system operates in a manner that reduces the number of full-resolution CNN convolution layers required in order to speed up the network inference and learning processes for the detection system. To do so, the detection system utilizes as an input a more compact representation of the tomographic data to alleviate the CNN memory footprint and computation time issues in prior art X-ray systems.
claims: 1. A method for detecting an anomaly in one or more images obtained from an X-ray imaging procedure on an object, the method comprising the steps of: a. providing a computing system on which an automatic anomaly detection system is provided, the anomaly detection system comprising: i. a processing unit configured to receive and process projection images, the processing unit including a convolutional neural network; ii. an electronic information storage device operably connected to the processing unit and containing operational instructions for the convolutional neural network; and b. supplying a compact data representation input of the projection images of an object of interest to the automatic anomaly detection system; c. analyzing the compact data representation input with the automatic anomaly detection system in at least one layer of the convolutional neural network to obtain an anomaly detection result; and d. outputting an anomaly detection result from the automatic anomaly detection system, wherein filters for the at least one layer of the convolutional neural network employed on the compact data representation input are at least partially learned by the automatic anomaly detection system. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the at least one layer of the convolutional neural network is one of a filtering operation, a reconstruction operation, a slabbing operation, a 2D synthetic image generation and combinations thereof. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the compact data representation input is the set of projection images. 4. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices. 5. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices; and c. performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered slabs. 6. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices; c. performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered slabs; and d. performing a synthetic 2D image generation operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered 2D synthetic images. 7. The method of claim 6 , further comprising the steps of: a. performing one or more detection operations on the filtered slabs to obtain a first lesion type output; and b. performing one or more detection convolution operations on the filtered 2D synthetic images to obtain a second lesion type output. 8. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices; and c. performing a synthetic 2D image generation operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered 2D synthetic images. 9. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input further comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a backprojection on the filtered projection images to form a backprojected filtered projection set; c. performing an aggregation operation on the filtered backprojected filtered projection set to form a slice image set; d. performing at least one of: i. a reconstruction operation on the slice image set to form one or more viewable slices, ii. a slabbing operation on the slice image set to form a slab image set and a convolutional operation on the slab image set to form one or more viewable slabs, or iii. combinations thereof; e. performing a detection operation on the slice image set or slab image set to form an anomaly detection result; and f. outputting the one or more viewable slices, the one or more viewable slabs, and combinations thereof in conjunction with the anomaly detection result. 10. The method of claim 1 , wherein the compact data representation input is a set of precomputed synthetic 2D image and further comprises a step of performing a filtering convolution operation on a synthetic 2D images to obtain filtered synthetic 2D images. 11. The method of claim 1 , wherein the compact data representation input is a set of precomputed slices obtained from the projection images. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of precomputed slices to form filtered slices; and b. performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to form filtered slabs. 13. The method of claim 1 , wherein the compact data representation input is a set of precomputed slabs and further comprises a step of performing a filtering operation on the set of slabs to form filtered slabs. 14. An X-ray imaging system comprising: a. an X-ray source configured to emit radiation beams towards an object; b. an X-ray detector movably aligned with the X-ray source to receive the radiation beams from the X-ray source and generate image data; c. a processing unit operably connected to the X-ray source and the X-ray detector to control the movement and operation of the X-ray source and X-ray detector, the processing unit configured to receive and process image data from the X-ray detector to form projection images; d. a display operably connected to the processing unit for presenting information to a user; e. a user interface operably connected to the processing unit to enable user input to the processing unit; f. an automatic anomaly detection system operably connected to the controller; and g. an electronic information storage device operably connected to the processing unit, wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is formed of a convolutional neural network having and operational instructions contained on the storage device to be accessed by the processing unit, the automatic anomaly detection system configured to receive a compact data representation input of the projection images and to analyze the compact data representation input to obtain an anomaly detection result, and wherein filters for the at least one layer of the convolutional neural network employed by the automatic anomaly detection system on the compact data representation are at least partially learned by the automatic anomaly detection system. 15. The method of claim 14 , wherein the at least one layer of the convolutional neural network is one of a filtering operation, a reconstruction operation, a slabbing operation, a 2D synthetic image generation and combinations thereof. 16. The X-ray imaging system of claim 14 , wherein the compact data representation input is the set of projection images. 17. The X-ray imaging system of claim 16 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to analyze the compact data representation input by performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images, and performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices. 18. The X-ray imaging system of claim 17 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to analyze the compact data representation input by performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered slabs. 19. The X-ray imaging system of claim 17 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to analyze the compact data representation input by performing a synthetic 2D image generation operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered 2D synthetic images. 20. The X-ray imaging system of claim 17 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to: a. analyze the compact data representation input by: i. performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered slabs; and ii. performing a synthetic 2D image generation operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered 2D synthetic image; and b. perform one or more detection operations on the filtered slabs to obtain a first lesion type output; and c. perform one or more detection operations on the filtered synthetic 2D to obtain a second lesion type output. 21. The X-ray imaging system of claim 16 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to: a. perform a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. perform a backprojection on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices; c. perform an aggregation operation on the filtered slices to form an 3D volume image set; d. perform at least one of a reconstruction operation to form a viewable slice volume from the 3D volume image set, and a slabbing operation to form a viewable slab volume from the 3D volume image set; e. perform a detection operation on the 3D volume image set to form an anomaly detection result; and f. output the viewable slices, viewable slabs, and combinations thereof in conjunction with the anomaly detection result on the display.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR TARGET IDENTIFICATION, ELECTRONIC APPARATUS, STORAGE MEDIUM AND NEUROMODULATION APPARATUS
abstract: The present disclosure provides a method and a device for target identification, an electronic apparatus, a storage medium and a neuromodulation apparatus. The method for target identification comprises: acquiring scanning data of a subject, wherein the scanning data comprise the data acquired from magnetic resonance imaging of the brain of the subject; determining at least two regions of interest of the subject based on the scanning data; determining at least one abnormal region of interest in the at least two regions of interest in accordance with a predetermined anomaly detection rule; determining a target based on the at least one abnormal region of interest.

title: METHOD FOR DETECTING ABNORMAL IMAGES IN A FIRST IMAGE DATASET USING ADVERSARIAL AUTOENCODERS; 用对抗自编码器检测第一图像数据集当中异常图像的方法
abstract:
引入了用于使用对抗自编码器（12,14,16）检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像的方法。在第一训练中利用图像训练数据集（11）来训练对抗自编码器（12,14,16），而优化对抗自编码器（12,14,16）使得图像训练数据集（11）的图像的潜在表示（13）的分布接近预定先验分布（17），并且最小化图像训练数据集的重构图像（15）的重构误差。在对对抗自编码器（12,14,16）的第一训练之后，在潜在表示（13）中检测异常，并且在第二训练中利用图像训练数据集（11）但是把检测到的异常考虑在内来训练对抗自编码器（12,14,16）。由经训练的对抗自编码器（12,14,16）取决于图像的重构误差和预定先验分布（17）下的概率密度中的至少一个来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像。

A method for detecting abnormal images in a first image dataset using an adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is introduced. In a first training, the adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is trained using the image training dataset (11), and the adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is optimized so that the distribution of the latent representation (13) of the images of the image training dataset (11) is close to a predetermined prior distribution (17), and the reconstruction error of the reconstructed image (15) of the image training dataset is minimized. After the first training of the adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16), anomalies are detected in the latent representation (13), and in a second training, the adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is trained using the image training dataset (11) but taking the detected anomalies into account. The abnormal images in the first image dataset are detected by the trained adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) depending on at least one of the reconstruction error of the image and the probability density under the predetermined prior distribution (17).
claims:
1 . 一种用于使用对抗自编码器来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像的方法，其特征在于： 在第一训练中利用图像训练数据集(11)训练对抗自编码器，而优化对抗自编码器使得图像训练数据集(11)的图像的潜在表示(13)的分布接近预定先验分布(17)，并且最小化图像训练数据集的重构图像(15)的重构误差， 在对对抗自编码器的第一训练之后，在图像训练数据集(11)的图像的潜在表示(13)中检测异常，并且在第二训练中利用图像训练数据集(11)但是把检测到的异常考虑在内来训练对抗自编码器， 由经训练的对抗自编码器取决于图像的重构误差和预定先验分布(17)下的概率密度中的至少一个来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像， 其中通过以下方式来把在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常考虑在内： 从用于第二训练的图像训练数据集(11)中排除检测到的异常，或者在至少一次后续训练中使用用于调谐对抗自编码器的加权损失函数，而在潜在表示 (13)中检测到的异常在所述至少一次后续训练中接收减小的权重，或者对于至少一次后续训练，将在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常集中每个异常的重构目标修改为其自身的噪声损坏版本，或者在至少一次后续训练中，将在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常集中每个异常的重构目标修改为潜在空间中标识为没有异常的靠近或最靠近的图像。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，潜在表示(13)中的异常的检测是取决于预期异常率来完成的，其中自适应异常检测阈值取决于预期异常率并且历史产出率提供对预期异常率的良好估计。 3 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，潜在表示(13)中的异常的检测是取决于它们的重构误差和预定先验分布(17)下它们的概率密度中的至少一个来完成的。 4 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，潜在表示(13)中的异常的检测是使用一类支持向量机或局部离群因子算法来完成的。 5 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，在一定数量的重复内相继重复地实施第一训练和第二训练，或相继重复地实施第一训练和第二训练直到达到训练目标。 6 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，第一训练包括第一预定数量的迭代。 7 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，第二训练包括第二预定数量的迭代。 8 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，预定先验分布包括至少一个专用拒绝类。 9 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，图像训练数据集(11)的图像最初是未标注的。 10 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，所述方法包括向用户的查询以将呈现的图像标识为正常或异常。 11 .根据权利要求10所述的方法，其特征在于，在第二训练中考虑在内的在潜在表示 (13)中检测到的异常包括用户标识的异常。 12 .根据权利要求10所述的方法，其特征在于，通过使用一类支持向量机或局部离群因子算法来完成潜在表示(13)中的异常的检测，并且而对于一类支持向量机或局部离群因子算法的训练，排除图像训练数据集(11)中用户标识为正常或异常的图像。 13 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，输出针对第一图像数据集中的图像的异常分数。 14 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，输出针对图像训练数据集 (11)中的图像的异常分数。 15 .根据权利要求10所述的方法，其特征在于，呈现给用户的图像是取决于它们的异常分数来选择的。 16 .根据权利要求15所述的方法，其特征在于，基于重构误差和预定先验分布(17)下的概率密度中的至少一个来计算异常分数。 17 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，它用于视觉品质检查、医学图像分析、视觉监视或自动化驾驶中。 18 . 一种计算机程序产品，包括计算机程序，其中计算机程序被处理器执行时适于施行根据权利要求1到16中的一项所述的方法。 19 . 一种计算机设备，适于施行根据权利要求1到16中的一项所述的方法。

solution_context: 引入了用于使用对抗自编码器（12,14,16）检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像的方法。 A method for detecting abnormal images in a first image dataset using an adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is introduced. 一种用于使用对抗自编码器来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像的方法，其特征在于： 在第一训练中利用图像训练数据集(11)训练对抗自编码器，而优化对抗自编码器使得图像训练数据集(11)的图像的潜在表示(13)的分布接近预定先验分布(17)，并且最小化图像训练数据集的重构图像(15)的重构误差， 在对对抗自编码器的第一训练之后，在图像训练数据集(11)的图像的潜在表示(13)中检测异常，并且在第二训练中利用图像训练数据集(11)但是把检测到的异常考虑在内来训练对抗自编码器， 由经训练的对抗自编码器取决于图像的重构误差和预定先验分布(17)下的概率密度中的至少一个来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像， 其中通过以下方式来把在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常考虑在内： 从用于第二训练的图像训练数据集(11)中排除检测到的异常，或者在至少一次后续训练中使用用于调谐对抗自编码器的加权损失函数，而在潜在表示 (13)中检测到的异常在所述至少一次后续训练中接收减小的权重，或者对于至少一次后续训练，将在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常集中每个异常的重构目标修改为其自身的噪声损坏版本，或者在至少一次后续训练中，将在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常集中每个异常的重构目标修改为潜在空间中标识为没有异常的靠近或最靠近的图像。

title: ANOMALY DETECTION METHOD, ANOMALY DETECTION PROGRAM, ANOMALY DETECTION DEVICE, SERVER DEVICE, AND INFORMATION PROCESSING METHOD; 异常检测方法、异常检测程序、异常检测装置、服务器装置以及信息处理方法
abstract:
计算机取得胸部X线图像(S50)，在取得的胸部X线图像中，使用模型检测边界线(S100)，模型在检测前使用机器学习构建而成，基于边界线，在取得的胸部X线图像中设定包含与心脏重叠的第1肺区域和与肝脏重叠的第2肺区域中的至少一方的第3肺区域(S200)，提取表示第3肺区域所包含的区域内存在的至少一个肺血管的粗细和密度中的至少一方的血管指标(S500)，基于血管指标和基准指标，判定第3肺区域所包含的区域是否为异常状态，基准指标使用了预先使用提取血管指标的方法从正常状态的胸部X线图像的与第3肺区域所包含的区域相当的区域提取出的指标(S700)，在判定为第3肺区域所包含的区域是异常状态时，输出表示判定的信息。

The computer obtains a chest X-ray image (S50). In the obtained chest X-ray image, a model is used to detect the boundary line (S100). The model is constructed using machine learning before detection. Based on the boundary line, the obtained chest X-ray image Set a third lung area including at least one of the first lung area overlapping the heart and the second lung area overlapping the liver (S200), and extract representing at least one lung present in the area included in the third lung area The blood vessel index of at least one of the thickness and density of the blood vessel (S500). Based on the blood vessel index and the reference index, it is determined whether the area included in the third lung area is abnormal. The index (S700) extracted from the area corresponding to the area included in the third lung area of the chest X-ray image of the state, when it is determined that the area included in the third lung area is an abnormal state, outputs information indicating the determination.

llm_reviewer_comment_short: 画像中の異常検出・分割とは近いが、医療・健康用途が主題で製造欠陥検出とは対象がずれる。
llm_reviewer_comment_detail: 課題文脈は欠陥・異常検出の目的を比較的よく表している。解決手段文脈は処理手順やモデル構成を読み取れる。技術手段は具体的なモデル・アルゴリズム名を含む。関連要素はあるものの、製造欠陥検出の代表例としては弱い。

title: GRAY AND WHITE MATTER VOLUME ABNORMALITY DETECTION AND CORRECTION METHOD, DEVICE, EQUIPMENT AND STORAGE MEDIUM; 灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法、装置、设备和存储介质
abstract:
本发明提出一种灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法，包括：基于行为学量表，获取测试对象的行为学评分结果，以及对应于所述行为学量表的行为学与脑结构之间的映射模板；将所述行为学评分结果与所述映射模板加权，获得加权模板；对测试对象的磁共振成像数据进行VBM分析，获得脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板；将所述加权模板与所述脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板比较，提取同时表现为显著异常的体素作为分析结果。本发明还公开了一种灰白质体积异常检测装置、一种/灰白质体积异常检测设备和一种计算机可读存储介质。采用本发明实施例，能够利用脑结构与行为学之间的关系，修正了由于VBM分析中多重比较误差所带来的假阳性。

The present invention proposes a gray and white matter volume abnormality detection and correction method, comprising: based on a behavioral scale, obtaining a behavioral scoring result of a test subject, and a mapping template between behavior and brain structure corresponding to the behavioral scale; weighting the behavioral scoring result and the mapping template to obtain a weighted template; performing VBM analysis on the magnetic resonance imaging data of the test subject to obtain a probability template of gray and white matter volume abnormality areas in the brain; comparing the weighted template with the probability template of gray and white matter volume abnormality areas in the brain, and extracting voxels that simultaneously show significant abnormalities as analysis results. The present invention also discloses a gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device, a gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device, and a computer-readable storage medium. By adopting the embodiments of the present invention, the relationship between brain structure and behavior can be utilized to correct false positives caused by multiple comparison errors in VBM analysis.
claims:
1 . 一种灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法，其特征在于，包括： 基于行为学量表，获取测试对象的行为学评分结果，以及对应于所述行为学量表的行为学与脑结构之间的映射模板； 将所述行为学评分结果与所述映射模板加权，获得加权模板； 对测试对象的磁共振成像数据进行VBM分析，获得脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板； 将所述加权模板与所述脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板比较，提取同时表现为显著异常的体素作为分析结果，从而修正VBM分析的多重比较误差所带来的假阳性； 其中，在获取所述映射模板之前，通过以下步骤构建所述映射模板，具体包括： 构建脑科学与神经病学的数据库； 利用所述数据库进行meta分析，构建对应于行为学量表的脑结构异常区域概率模板作为映射模板。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述脑结构异常区域概率模板的每个体素为从行为学量表提取的特征分数与各脑区的相关系数。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述脑结构异常区域概率模板根据所述数据库及其分析和所述行为学量表的更新而更新。 4 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述数据库包括脑科学与神经病学的实验数据、研究结论、文献和指南中的至少一种。 5 . 一种灰白质体积异常检测装置，其特征在于，包括： 输入单元，用于获取测试对象磁共振图像数据，以及行为学评分结果； 存储单元，用于存储对应于各种行为学量表的各种行为学与脑结构之间的映射模板； 数据处理单元，用于对所述磁共振成像数据进行VBM分析，获得脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板，并将所述行为学评分结果与所述映射模板加权，获得加权模板，以及将所述加权模板与所述脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板比较，提取同时表现为显著异常的体素作为分析结果，从而修正VBM分析的多重比较误差所带来的假阳性； 其中，所述行为学与脑结构之间的映射模板通过对脑科学与神经病学的数据库进行 meta分析得出。 6 .如权利要求5所述的灰白质体积异常检测装置，其特征在于，所述灰白质体积异常检测装置还包括： 检测输出单元，用于将分析得到的脑部灰白质体积异常信号按照体积大小进行排序， 并输出检测报告；其中，所述脑部灰白质体积异常信号的体积由异常区域总体素数乘以图像分辨率计算得出。 7 . 一种灰白质体积异常检测设备，其特征在于，包括处理器、存储器以及存储在所述存储器中且被配置为由所述处理器执行的计算机程序，所述处理器执行所述计算机程序时实现如权利要求1至4中任意一项所述的灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法。 8 .一种计算机可读存储介质，其特征在于，所述计算机可读存储介质包括存储的计算机程序，其中，在所述计算机程序运行时控制所述计算机可读存储介质所在设备执行如权利要求1至4中任意一项所述的灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法。

1 . A method for detecting and correcting abnormal gray and white matter volumes, characterized in that it includes: based on a behavioral scale, obtaining the behavioral scoring results of the test subject, and a mapping template between the behavior and brain structure corresponding to the behavioral scale; weighting the behavioral scoring results and the mapping template to obtain a weighted template; performing VBM analysis on the magnetic resonance imaging data of the test subject to obtain a probability template for abnormal brain gray and white matter volume areas; comparing the weighted template with the probability template for abnormal brain gray and white matter volume areas, and extracting voxels that simultaneously show significant abnormalities as analysis results, thereby correcting false positives caused by multiple comparison errors of VBM analysis; wherein, before obtaining the mapping template, the mapping template is constructed through the following steps, specifically including: constructing a database of brain science and neurology; using the database to perform meta-analysis, and constructing a probability template for abnormal brain structure areas corresponding to the behavioral scale as a mapping template. 2 .The method according to claim 1 is characterized in that each voxel of the probability template of the abnormal brain structure area is the correlation coefficient between the feature score extracted from the behavioral scale and each brain area. 3 .The method according to claim 1 is characterized in that the probability template of the abnormal brain structure area is updated based on the database and its analysis and the update of the behavioral scale. 4 .The method according to claim 1 is characterized in that the database includes at least one of experimental data, research conclusions, literature and guidelines in brain science and neurology. 5 . A gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device, characterized in that it includes: an input unit for acquiring magnetic resonance image data of a test subject, and a behavioral scoring result; a storage unit for storing mapping templates between various behaviors and brain structures corresponding to various behavioral scales; a data processing unit for performing VBM analysis on the magnetic resonance imaging data to obtain a probability template of abnormal brain gray and white matter volume areas, and weighting the behavioral scoring result with the mapping template to obtain a weighted template, and comparing the weighted template with the probability template of abnormal brain gray and white matter volume areas to extract voxels that simultaneously show significant abnormalities as analysis results, thereby correcting false positives caused by multiple comparison errors of VBM analysis; wherein the mapping template between behaviors and brain structures is obtained by performing a meta-analysis on databases of brain science and neurology. 6 .The gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device as described in claim 5 is characterized in that the gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device also includes: a detection output unit, which is used to sort the analyzed brain gray and white matter volume abnormality signals according to volume size and output a detection report; wherein the volume of the brain gray and white matter volume abnormality signal is calculated by multiplying the total prime number of the abnormal area by the image resolution. 7 . A gray matter volume abnormality detection device, characterized in that it includes a processor, a memory, and a computer program stored in the memory and configured to be executed by the processor, and when the processor executes the computer program, the gray matter volume abnormality detection and correction method as described in any one of claims 1 to 4 is implemented. 8 .A computer-readable storage medium, characterized in that the computer-readable storage medium includes a stored computer program, wherein when the computer program is running, the device where the computer-readable storage medium is located is controlled to execute the gray-white matter volume abnormality detection and correction method as described in any one of claims 1 to 4.
