title: UNMANNED AIRCRAFT SYSTEM, METHOD FOR IDENTIFYING AND REGISTERING ASSETS AND MONITORING AND INSPECTING POWER TRANSMISSION AND DISTRIBUTION LINES; SYSTÈME DE VÉHICULE AÉRIEN SANS PILOTE, PROCÉDÉ D'IDENTIFICATION ET D'ENREGISTREMENT D'ACTIFS, DE SURVEILLANCE ET D'INSPECTION DE LIGNES DE TRANSMISSION ET DE DISTRIBUTION D'ÉNERGIE; SISTEMA DE VEÍCULO AÉREO NÃO TRIPULADO, MÉTODO DE IDENTIFICAÇÃO E CADASTRO DE ATIVOS, MONITORAMENTO E INSPEÇÃO DE LINHAS DE TRANSMISSÃO E DE DISTRIBUIÇÃO DE ENERGIA
abstract:
The invention relates to an unmanned aircraft system (UAS) comprising a robotic platform, an automatic charging subsystem, an intelligent integrated management subsystem, an on-board intelligence module and command and control stations. In addition, the invention also defines a method for identifying and registering assets and monitoring and inspecting power transmission and distribution lines which comprises the steps of automatically identifying and differentiating assets remotely and using the information collected to generate real-time situational analysis reports and asset loss prospecting and maintenance reports. This makes the processes, database and management of maintenance resources more effective.

L'invention concerne un système de véhicule aérien sans pilote (UAV) qui comprend une plateforme robotique, un sous-système de chargement automatique, un sous-système intelligent de gestion intégrée, un module d'intelligence embarquée et des postes de commande et de contrôle. De plus, l'invention concerne également un procédé d'identification et d'enregistrement d'actifs, de surveillance et d'inspection de lignes de transmission et de distribution d'énergie qui comprend les étapes d'identification et de différenciation automatique à distance des actifs et l'utilisation des informations recueillies pour générer des rapports d'analyse de situation en temps réel et de prospection de pertes d'actifs, ainsi que des rapports d'entretien associés. L'efficacité des processus, de la base de données et la gestion des ressources de manutention sont ainsi mises en valeur.

Sistema de Veículo Aéreo Não Tripulado (SVANT) que compreende uma plataforma robótica, um subsistema de carregamento automático, um subsistema inteligente de gestão integrada, um módulo de inteligência embarcada e Estações de Comando e Controle. Além disso, a invenção também define um método de identificação e cadastro de ativos, monitoramento e Inspeção de Linhas de Transmissão e de distribuição de Energia que compreende as etapas de identificação e diferenciação automática remota dos ativos e uso das informações coletadas para gerar relatórios da análise situacional em tempo real e prospecção de perdas de ativos bem como relatórios para manutenção destes. Sendo assim, favorece a efetividade dos processos, da base de dados e a gestão dos recursos de manutenção.
claims:
REIVINDICAÇÕES1. Sistema de Veículo Aéreo Não Tripulado (VANT) caracterizado por compreender uma plataforma robótica, um sistema de carregamento automático, um subsistema inteligente de gestão integrada, um módulo de inteligência embarcada e Estações de Comando e Controle. 2. Sistema de Veículo Aéreo Não Tripulado (VANT), de acordo com a reivindicação 1 , caracterizado por ser utilizável por diversos tipos de baterias em diferentes plataformas robóticas. 3. Sistema de Veículo Aéreo Não Tripulado (VANT), de acordo com a reivindicação 1, caracterizado pelo fato de a plataforma robótica compreender um sistema de geoposicionamento, câmeras, sensores optrônicos (como RGB, térmico, Corona), sistema anticolisão, sistema de escaneamento a laser, computador embarcado e enlaces de comunicação de dados digitais. 4. DESCRIPTIVE REPORT Application for an Invention Patent for “UNMANNED AERIAL VEHICLE SYSTEM, METHOD FOR IDENTIFYING AND REGISTERING ASSETS, MONITORING AND INSPECTING ENERGY TRANSMISSION AND DISTRIBUTION LINES” FIELD OF INVENTION [001] The invention is multidisciplinary in nature, involving the fields of Aeronautical, Computer, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, in the development of tools for the inspection of power transmission and distribution lines, more specifically an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle System (UAV) for registering assets, their maintenance and network inspection, featuring an automatic recharging system and an Intelligent Integrated Management System. STATE OF THE TECHNIQUE [002] Currently, in the high and low voltage distribution area, images are collected for analysis by the operator, so that, at a later stage, defects can be analyzed via software. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are mainly used to collect images over long distances. [003] In addition, there are also projects to carry out visual inspections on distribution lines. However, in these studies, the robots are fixed to the line and/or do not allow for the collection of 3D images, or even with a geopositioning system being processed in real time, which in a certain way increases the accuracy of the images and the quality of the information obtained. [004] The self-loading UAV developed performs flights close to distribution and transmission networks, collecting georeferenced images in the visible and/or multispectral spectrum, such as thermal images with radiometry. The collection of images allows the 3D mapping of the asset, analyzing it, identifying it, registering it in the sequence and, at the same time, identifying possible defects. [005] 3D analysis, automatic registration of assets in real time, mapping of vegetation growth, with an Intelligent Integrated Management System, is an innovative approach not yet carried out in R&D projects in Brazil. [006] In summary, the system and method developed work with analysis algorithms and automatic identification of defects, inconsistencies, diagnosis and application of artificial intelligence for asset registration using 3D mapping, vegetation growth mapping and self-charging of the UAV using distribution and transmission lines. [007] Patent application PI 0904898-7, of the same ownership as the present application, discloses a Multipurpose Aerial Monitoring Platform that allows the controller to remotely control an aircraft or other robotic devices through a command and control station. [008] The multipurpose aerial monitoring platform has several applications in the military and civil areas, such as aerial monitoring of high voltage transmission lines, roads, gas pipelines, urban areas, topographic surveys, various mapping, such as environmental and risk areas, and also as a development platform for robotic, aeronautical and similar systems where a mobile and versatile aerial platform is required, which allows or does not allow its integration with other technological systems. [009] However, it is worth noting that the aforementioned state of the art document does not reveal or suggest a self-charging system, with 3D mapping, vegetation growth mapping and corona effects analysis. [010] Document BR 10 2015 032214 3 refers to an image generation system, with global georeferenced registration, obtained from a small UAV. The use of such a system allows the use of small aircraft to map and monitor areas of the Earth's surface. [011] The invention of document BR 10 2015 032214 3 comprises the development and construction of a prototype of an aircraft specially designed to contain the components related to the mapping and monitoring application, as well as the image processing system dedicated to the global georeferenced recording of the images obtained from the aircraft, without the use of a stabilization and balance device and with the storage of the generated images and post-processing at the control station, using the image mosaic reconstruction technique and subsequent conversion into a georeferenced mosaic. [012] Document BR 10 2015 032214 3 does not reveal a self-loading UAV (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) System that performs automatic remote registration of assets and uses the information collected to generate asset loss reports as well as reports for their maintenance. [013] Document CN114604110A discloses an unmanned aerial vehicle wireless charging control system and method; and the system comprises an unmanned aerial vehicle comprising an unmanned aerial vehicle coil; the charging platform comprises a parking apron, a plurality of power transmission coils, and a plurality of induction detection coils. [014] An induction detection coil coincides with a power transmission coil, and each induction detection coil is connected to a voltage detector; After the unmanned aerial vehicle lands on the parking lot, the coil of the unmanned aerial vehicle is coupled to all the induction detection coils, the energy is wirelessly transmitted to all the induction detection coils, and then the position of the unmanned aerial vehicle is determined according to the voltage value of each induction detection coil. [015] The power transmission coil corresponding to the induction detection coil with the maximum voltage detection value wirelessly transmits power to the coil of the unmanned aerial vehicle so as to charge the unmanned aerial vehicle; the position of the unmanned aerial vehicle does not need to be moved by adopting a centering mechanism. [016] Wireless power transmission is realized for the unmanned aerial vehicle by discovering a power transmission coil which is arranged in a parking lot and has the largest coupling degree with a coil of unmanned aerial vehicle, and the charging efficiency is improved; when the induction detection coil works. Only a small current is needed, and electric energy is saved. [017] Document CN114604110A also does not disclose a self-loading UAV (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) System that performs automatic remote asset registration and uses the information collected to generate asset loss reports as well as reports for asset maintenance. [018] The self-loading UAV (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) System developed has two main purposes: monitoring for the survey and automated registration of assets, such as transformers, insulators, cables, poles, public lighting and other assets and their respective inspections in distribution and transmission lines. [019] Additionally, through an Intelligent Integrated Management System (SIGI), the UAV will use the information collected to generate reports of the real situational analysis found by the robotic platform during mapping. Such reports may reference asset losses as well as reports for their maintenance. For the UAV to be able to fulfill the two listed purposes, a self-charging system will enable an increase in efficiency, allowing for increased autonomy in operation that will be greater in relation to the systems available on the market. [020] Thus, the proposed development reduces costs in the asset registration process. The innovations proposed in this patent application are directly related to the UAV self-loading system, the vegetative control of easement areas and the like, and the automatic asset registration. [021] Furthermore, for the asset registration to be carried out, Computer Vision and Artificial Intelligence algorithms (also related to Big Data) are applied to process, analyze and inform what the data is. [022] The proposed development considers low voltage power distribution systems and high voltage transmission lines. DESIGNS [023] Figure 1 shows the basic diagram of the Intelligent Integrated Management System (SIGI) software with the interfaces and relationships between the devices. [024] Figure 2 illustrates the schematic diagram of the developed system, where it is possible to observe the receiving and transmitting coils of the circuit. [025] Figure 3 shows the BRV-03 robotic platform, where the data connections, anti-collision system, front camera and GPS are indicated. [026] Figure 4 shows the Mobile Command and Control Station, developed on a vehicle platform, being fully customized for this operation. [027] Figure 5 shows the Portable Command Station, developed on a portable platform, being installed at the location of interest to provide local infrastructure for the operation. DETAILED DESCRIPTION OF THE INVENTION [028] The invention describes a Multipurpose Aerial Monitoring Platform (UAV), for registering assets and inspecting energy transmission and distribution lines, with an automatic recharging system and an Intelligent Integrated Management System. [029] The Automatic Power Line Inspection tool seeks to solve the problem of maintenance inspection and management of the structure of Power Lines and related assets, where the UAV and related tools, for example, SIGI management software, Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision procedures assist predictive maintenance procedures, increasing the robustness of the electrical network. [030] The development of the tool aims to solve the problem of inspection maintenance and management of the structure of voltage line assets, automatically, using robotic platforms to fly over selected areas and perform data acquisition and processing. [031] Furthermore, it is worth noting that the systems are monitored by dedicated software running within the Command and Control Station (CCS), to increase the effectiveness of this technology. [032] The entire process is monitored and controlled by customizable software called the Intelligent Integrated Management System (SIGI), an integrated enterprise management system (ERP). The SIGI software can manage data, connect sensors (IoT) and analyze results according to authority requests. [033] Based on the data collected from the areas of interest, the SIGI software shows in real time the situational analysis of these areas and allows the administrator to optimize resources (material and human) improving the efficiency of resource allocation in these areas. [034] In addition to the development of management software, the invention relates to the development of sensors to collect information in the field and update this information to the management software database. [035] Furthermore, the invention relates to the development of a wireless robotic platform charging station, such as a drone, navigation and control methodology using Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision based on COTS (Commercial-Of-The-Shelf) equipment and management software. [036] The developed system has the addition of technologies that refine the self-charging process and make it intelligent, making it possible to use different types of batteries on different robotic platforms. [037] Such technologies involve an intelligent and adaptive microprocessor circuit, which identifies the type of robotic platform, automatically configures the autocharger, and internet connectivity, enabling the user to remotely control and monitor the recharging of the robotic platform. [038] The robotic platform developed has a modular architecture, based on COTS (Commercial-of-the-Shelf) equipment, that is, off-the-shelf equipment, where it is possible to include Corona sensors, without the systemic decharacterization of the equipment. [039] In addition, the system also has embedded intelligence, through the use of Artificial Intelligence, for the recognition and identification of assets, in a parameterized and regionalized manner. In a complementary manner, the methods used post-process the data to obtain greater assertiveness and improve the intended results. [040] The developed multipurpose monitoring platform is an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) system. In this sense, it is worth highlighting that the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) system comprises, as main elements, a robotic platform, an automatic charging system, an intelligent integrated management subsystem, an on-board intelligence module and a Command and Control Station. [041] The robotic platform has a modular design and uses a COTS (Commercial-off-the-Shelf) concept for simple maintenance and component replacement. The structure is made of composite materials, which makes it strong and lightweight. The robotic platforms are capable of performing small internal data processing, such as obtaining images, georeferencing and identifying objects, scanning/mapping areas, redirecting mapping, and other functions involving machine learning. In other words, the robotic platform is interchangeable with various applications and performs operational redirection according to demand. [042] The robotic platform comprises a geopositioning system, which may be of the GPS type - RTK/PPK, 4x RGB Camera, optronic sensors (such as RGB, thermal, Corona), Thermal Camera, NIR camera, Anti-collision system, laser scanning system, Onboard Computer (Intelligent robotic platform), 4G LTE Communication (BVLOS), where the sensors can be customized according to the application and new market demands. [043] The automatic charging system is carried out by transmitting electrical energy from one point to another through a vacuum or atmosphere without the use of electrical wires or any other contact material. [044] Wireless power transmission (WPT) is the process that occurs in any system where electromagnetic (EM) energy is transmitted from a power source to an electrical load, without artificial interconnecting conductors. [045] Robotic platforms can be equipped with various devices that use electricity, such as sensors, high-resolution and infrared cameras, tracking and the global positioning system. They can be used as equipment for monitoring agricultural fields, railway lines and power transmission lines. [046] In the developed system, the battery can be charged at the base station of the robotic platform, through a self-inductance system. In addition, it is carried out with a circuit model using an induction coil with a low-frequency core to power batteries through wireless charging with the aim of supplying energy to the battery of a robotic platform. [047] The intelligent integrated management subsystem (SIGI) is responsible for the entire process of monitoring the inspection and management of the power line asset structure. SIGI can manage data, connect sensors (loT) and analyze the results according to the authority's requests. Furthermore, it is worth noting that the intelligent integrated management subsystem comprises a multi-user and multi-system Interface Module. [048] Based on the data collected from the areas of interest, SIGI shows in real time the situational analysis of these areas and allows the administrator to optimize resources (material and human) improving the efficiency of resource allocation in these areas. In addition to the development of management software, the development of sensors to collect information in the field and update this information to the management software database is planned. [049] As seen in Figure 1, the basic SIGI diagram relates to the interfaces and devices, in addition to comprising: [050] SIGI main module: This software is designed as an ERP software, with a modular concept that allows customization of functionalities according to the application. The main block of this module contains the main ERP software, with web access, database, connectivity with Drone software and APP software. [051] Interface Module: Allows SIGI connectivity with field Command and Control Centers (C2), such as the Camera Monitoring Room, ground team connectivity, and communicates with any type of infrastructure, whether local or remote, with internet access. [052] Field Module: Represents the devices that will be used in the field, with the collection of information generated by sensors (loT), robotic platforms, customized according to the mission profile. [053] Operational results: The diagram shown in Figure 1 represents the results of the application, in terms of reports, predictive models and big data, to assist managers in decision making. In this case, the operational results will be obtained according to systemic customizations, according to the definition of the object to be managed. [054] Robotic platform: In this example, of the Quadcopter type (which can be varied according to the operational demand, from aerial, surface and terrestrial platforms), shown in Figure 3, it acquires images and executes artificial intelligence procedures of the inspected areas. [055] In addition, the UAV System comprises the onboard intelligence module, which is developed through the use of Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision, for the recognition and identification of assets, in a parameterized and regionalized manner. [056] Furthermore, the Command and Control Stations stand out, such as the Mobile Command and Control Vehicle, in Figure 4, and the DroneBox, in Figure 5. The Mobile Command and Control Vehicle involves the development of computing devices mounted on a mobile platform, capable of constantly monitoring the operation of the robotic platform, collecting data in the field and interfacing with the Integrated Command and Control Center, through data links, such as the internet. The same feature applies to DroneBox, but as a main feature, DroneBox is used in a fixed and punctual way. [057] The Command and Control Station (CCS) monitors the Unmanned Aerial System in real time, from takeoff to landing. [058] Furthermore, the proposed invention also defines a method for remote automatic registration of assets, such as transformers, insulators, cables and other assets and inspections on distribution lines, posts (mutual use) and public lighting. Furthermore, the method allows, through an Intelligent Integrated Management System (SIGI), the use of the information collected to generate reports on asset losses as well as reports for the maintenance of these assets. [059] Thus, it is clarified that the developed method performs data acquisition in the field, the embedded processing (Embedded Intelligence), in a complementary manner, performs post-processing of the data in the integrated management system (SIGI), where there is an interface with the data from the Distributor's Geographic Database (BDGD) and, in a comparative manner, performs data analysis, generating specific maintenance reports, asset losses and illustrates the efficiency of the tool, through statistical data.

title: SELF-ATTENTION-BASED METHOD FOR DETECTING DEFECTIVE AREA OF COLOR-TEXTURED FABRIC; 一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法; PROCÉDÉ À BASE D'AUTO-ATTENTION PERMETTANT DE DÉTECTER UNE ZONE DÉFECTUEUSE DE TISSU À TEXTURE COLORÉE
abstract:
A self-attention-based method for detecting a defective area of a color-textured fabric. The method specifically comprises: establishing a color-textured fabric data set which comprises a color texture defect-free image, and superimposing noise on the color texture defect-free image in the color-textured fabric data set; constructing a Transformer-based Swin-Unet model and training same to obtain a trained model; and reconstructing, by using the trained model, a color-textured fabric image to be subjected to detection, outputting a corresponding reconstructed image, and then determining and positioning a defective area according to the reconstructed image. By means of the method, the problem of the detection of a defective area of a color-textured fabric can be effectively solved.

Procédé à base d'auto-attention permettant de détecter une zone défectueuse d'un tissu à texture colorée. Le procédé consiste spécifiquement : à établir un ensemble de données de tissu à texture colorée qui comprend une image sans défaut de texture colorée et à superposer du bruit sur l'image sans défaut de texture colorée dans l'ensemble de données de tissu à texture colorée ; à construire un modèle de Swin-Unet basé sur un transformateur et à l'instruire pour obtenir un modèle instruit ; et à reconstruire, à l'aide du modèle instruit, une image de tissu à texture colorée à soumettre à détection, à transmettre une image reconstruite correspondante, puis à déterminer et à positionner une zone défectueuse selon l'image reconstruite. Grâce au procédé, on peut résoudre efficacement le problème de détection d'une zone défectueuse d'un tissu à texture colorée.

一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法,具体为:建立包括彩色纹理无缺陷图像的彩色纹理织物数据集,对彩色纹理织物数据集中的彩色纹理无缺陷图像叠加噪声;构建基于Transformer的Swin-Unet模型并训练得到训练好的模型;使用训练好的模型对待测彩色纹理织物图像进行重构,输出对应的重构图像,然后根据重构图像判断并定位缺陷区域。该方法能够有效地解决彩色纹理织物缺陷区域检测的问题。
claims:
一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法,其特征在于,具体按照如下步骤实施: 步骤1,建立包括彩色纹理无缺陷图像的彩色纹理织物数据集,对彩色纹理织物数据集中的彩色纹理无缺陷图像叠加噪声; 步骤2,构建基于Transformer的Swin-Unet模型; 步骤3,将步骤1叠加噪声的彩色纹理织物无缺陷图像输入到步骤2构建的基于Transformer的Swin-Unet模型中进行训练,得到训练好的基于Transformer的Swin-Unet模型; 步骤4,使用步骤3训练好的基于Transformer的Swin-Unet模型对待测彩色纹理织物图像进行重构,输出对应的重构图像,然后根据重构图像判断并定位缺陷区域。 根据权利要求1所述的一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法,其特征在于,所述步骤1具体为: 步骤1.1,建立彩色纹理织物数据集,彩色纹理织物数据包括彩色纹理织物无缺陷图像训练集和彩色纹理织物有缺陷图像测试集,彩色纹理织物数据集中的所有图像均整理成512×512×3大小的分辨率,图像格式均为.jpg; 步骤1.2,对步骤1.1中的彩色纹理织物无缺陷图像训练集中的彩色纹理织物无缺陷图像叠加噪声,如式(1)所示: 式中,X为彩色纹理织物无缺陷图像,N(0,0.1)表示服从均值为0、方差为0.1的标准正态分布的高斯噪声, 为叠加噪声后的彩色纹理织物无缺陷图像。 根据权利要求2所述的一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法,其特征在于,所述步骤2中的基于Transformer的Swin-Unet模型具体为: 所述基于Transformer的Swin-Unet模型为一个基于Transformer的U型对称编码器-解码器结构,分别由编码器、瓶颈层、解码器依次连接组成,编码器的输入层为叠加噪声彩色纹理织物无缺陷图像,解码器的输出层为重构后的彩色纹理织物图像,编码器和解码器之间通过3个跳接层相互连接。 根据权利要求3所述的一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法,其特征在于,所述编码器由输入层、Patch Embedding层、3个Swin Transformer Block层和3个Patch Merging层连接组成,其中,Swin Transformer Block层和Patch Merging层交替相连,所述Patch Embedding层利用卷积核为4、步长为4、填充为0的卷积与Swin Transformer Block层相连,所述Swin Transformer Block层利用自注意力层与在Swin Transformer Block层之后的Patch Merging层相连,其中,自注意力层包括在所述Swin Transformer Block层中,所述Patch Merging层利用全连接层和通道归一化操作与在Patch Merging层之后的Swin Transformer Block层相连,其中,全连接层和通道归一化操作包括在所述Patch Merging层中,编码器的最后一个所述Patch Merging层与所述瓶颈层连接; 所述瓶颈层由2个Swin Transformer Block层依次连接组成,编码器的输出层通过通道归一化操作连接到瓶颈层的第一个Swin Transformer Block层,其中,通道归一化操作包括在所述编码器的输出层中,瓶颈层的第二个Swin Transformer Block层通过全连接层连接到解码器的输入层,其中,全连接层包括在所述第二个Swin Transformer Block层中; 所述解码器由3个Patch Expanding层、3个Swin Transformer Block层、Patch Projection层、输出层连接组成,解码器的第一个所述Patch Expanding层与所述瓶颈层的第二个所述Swin Transformer Block层连接,在解码器中,Patch Expanding层和Swin Transformer Block层交替相连,所述Patch Expanding层利用全连接层和通道归一化操作与Swin Transformer Block层相连,所述Swin Transformer Block层利用自注意力层与Patch Projection层连接,所述Patch Projection层利用卷积核为1、步长为1、填充为0的卷积与输出层相连; 所述编码器的3个Swin Transformer Block层与解码器的3个Swin Transformer Block层一一对应连接。 根据权利要求4所述的一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法,其特征在于,所述Swin Transformer Block层由LayerNorm层、窗口多头自注意力层、移位窗口多头自注意力层、MLP层组成,所述LayerNorm层是通道归一化操作,所述窗口多头自注意力层和移位窗口多头自注意力层都由2个全连接层组成,并在全连接层后加入了激活函数Softmax,移位窗口多头自注意力层在激活函数Softmax后加入了移位和切片操作,所述MLP层由2个全连接层组成,并在2个全连接层间加入了激活函数GELU,按照以下方式连接: 输入特征z l-1 先经过LayerNorm层,再经过窗口多头自注意力层,接着经过相加操作得到 接着经过LayerNorm层、MLP层和相加操作得到z l ,然后再经过LN层、移位窗口多头自注意力层和相加操作得到 最后经过LayerNorm层、MLP层和相加操作得到输出特征z l+1 ,过程如式(2): 式中,LN()表示经过LayerNorm层处理输出,MLP()表示经过MLP层处理输出,W-MSA()表示经过窗口多头自注意力层处理输出、SW-MSA()表示经过移位窗口多头自注意力层处理输出,LayerNorm层为通道归一化操作。 根据权利要求5所述的一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法,其特征在于,所述窗口多头自注意力层和移位窗口多头自注意力层是在每个窗口中计算自注意力Attention(Q,K,V),如式(3): 式中,Q、K、V分别表示查询矩阵、键矩阵、值矩阵,d表示矩阵的维度,B表示偏置矩阵,SoftMax为激活函数。 根据权利要求6所述的一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法,其特征在于,所述编码器的第一个Swin Transformer Block层中MLP层神经元个数前者为48、后者为192,编码器的第二个Swin Transformer Block层中MLP层神经元个数前者为96、后者为384,编码器的第三个Swin Transformer Block层中MLP层神经元个数前者为192、后者为768,所述瓶颈层的Swin Transformer Block层中MLP层神经元个数前者和后者分别都为384和1536,所述解码器的每个Swin Transformer Block层中MLP层神经元个数与编码器的MLP层中相对应的神经元个数相等。 根据权利要求7所述的一种基于自注意力的彩色纹理织物缺陷区域的检测方法,其特征在于,所述步骤3具体为: 步骤3.1,将叠加噪声的彩色纹理织物无缺陷图像输入到步骤2构建的基于Transformer的Swin-Unet模型中得到重构图像; title: METHOD AND PROCESS FOR AUTOMATIC INSPECTION OF LARGE-SCALE SOLAR PHOTOVOLTAIC (PV) POWER PLANTS BASED ON MULTI-VIEW AERIAL PHOTOGRAMMETRY AND COOPERATIVE INTELLIGENCE MODELS; PROCÉDÉ ET MÉTHODE POUR L'INSPECTION AUTOMATIQUE DES CENTRALES SOLAIRES PHOTOVOLTAÏQUES (PV) À LARGE ÉCHELLE À BASE DE LA PHOTOGRAMMÉTRIE AÉRIENNE MULTI-VUES ET DES MODÈLES COOPÉRATIFS D'INTELLIGENCE
abstract:
La présente description concerne un procédé dinspection des centrales photovoltaïques à large échelle à base de la photogrammétrie aérienne multi-vues par caméras visuelles et thermiques ; et une méthode de détection multi-niveaux des défauts des panneaux photovoltaïques par des modèles coopératifs dintelligence artificielle par apprentissage profond. Le procédé comprend les étapes depuis la planification de la mission dinspection par la plateforme aérienne, à la détection et la géolocalisation précise des défauts, jusquà la génération du rapport dinspection géospatial et visualisation dans un géoportail. Le procédé assure aux opérateurs des interventions localisées, rapides et optimisées en termes de temps et de ressources.

The present description relates to a method for inspecting large-scale photovoltaic power plants based on multi-view aerial photogrammetry by visual and thermal cameras; and a method for multi-level detection of defects in photovoltaic panels by cooperative artificial intelligence models using deep learning. The process includes the steps from planning the inspection mission by the aerial platform, to the detection and precise geolocation of defects, to the generation of the geospatial inspection report and visualization in a geoportal. The process ensures operators localized, rapid and optimized interventions in terms of time and resources.

problem_context: La présente description concerne un procédé dinspection des centrales photovoltaïques à large échelle à base de la photogrammétrie aérienne multi-vues par caméras visuelles et thermiques ; et une méthode de détection multi-niveaux des défauts des panneaux photovoltaïques par des modèles coopératifs dintelligence artificielle par apprentissage profond.
solution_context: The present description relates to a method for inspecting large-scale photovoltaic power plants based on multi-view aerial photogrammetry by visual and thermal cameras; and a method for multi-level detection of defects in photovoltaic panels by cooperative artificial intelligence models using deep learning. The process includes the steps from planning the inspection mission by the aerial platform, to the detection and precise geolocation of defects, to the generation of the geospatial inspection report and visualization in a geoportal.
